February 5, 2025 
COVID-19 & Antimicrobial Resistance 
Key points 
The U.S. lost progress in combating antimicrobial resistance (AR) in 2020 because of COVID-19. 
COVID-19 impacts on antimicrobial resistance 
The U.S. lost progress in combating antimicrobial resistance (AR) in 2020 due, in large part, to effects of the COVID-19 pandemic. The CDC COVID-19: U.S. Impact on Antimicrobial Resistance, Special Report 2022 , concluded that the threat of antimicrobial-resistant infections was not only still present but had gotten worse. 
During the first year of the pandemic, more than 29,400 people died from antimicrobial-resistant infections commonly associated with health care. Of these, nearly 40% of the people got the infection while they were in the hospital.
